2605.21108.title: Efficient Learning of Deep State Space Models via Importance Smoothing
2605.21108.paper_summary: Latent state space systems are ubiquitous in statistical modelling, arising naturally when a time series is observed through a noisy measurement function, however training deep state space models (DSSM) at scale remains difficult. Two largely distinct strategies and literatures have developed around the training of DSSMs. Firstly, auto-encoding DSSMs train generative DSSMs by optimising a variational lower bound. Secondly, DSSMs trained by back-propagating the outputs of a classical sequential Monte Carlo algorithm (SMC). Such approaches can train DSSMs for discriminative as well as generative tasks, however, due to the sequentiality of their forward pass, scale poorly on modern hardware. We propose a new training method \emph{parallel variational Monte Carlo} (PVMC) that bridges the gap between the paradigms, and can be used robustly to train DSSMs for both discriminative and generative tasks. Our method achieves state-of-the-art or better results on a set of baseline experiments and trains $10\times$ faster than the fastest competing SMC approach.
2605.21108.paper_summary_zh: 潛在狀態空間系統在統計建模中無處不在，當時間序列通過噪聲測量函數觀察時，自然產生，然而在大規模訓練深度狀態空間模型（DSSM）仍然困難。圍繞DSSM的訓練發展了兩種大致不同的策略和文獻。首先，自編碼DSSM通過優化變分下界來訓練生成性DSSM。其次，DSSM通過反向傳播經典序列蒙特卡洛算法（SMC）的輸出進行訓練。這些方法可以訓練DSSM以應對判別性和生成性任務，然而，由於其前向傳播的序列性，在現代硬體上擴展性差。我們提出了一種新的訓練方法\emph{平行變分蒙特卡洛}（PVMC），它彌合了這兩種範式之間的差距，並且可以穩健地用於訓練DSSM以應對判別性和生成性任務。我們的方法在一組基準實驗中達到了最先進或更好的結果，並且訓練速度比最快的競爭性SMC方法快$10\times$。
2605.21108.author: John-Joseph Brady et.al.
2605.21108.authors: John-Joseph Brady, Nikolas Nusken, Yunpeng Li
